title: Constantia concept catalogue
description: A concept is a repo-agnostic design principle worth defending, expressed in prose. Rules (separate file) attach selectors + investigations to it.

principle.description: The design principle itself, in prose. This is what the LLM investigator reads side-by-side with each matched file.

rationale.description: Why violating this principle hurts. Surfaces in reports and reviewer docs.

discovery.description: Optional author-time aid. A free-text question an author can run through their codebase-exploration tool of choice to surface representative canonical and violating files, which then inform the selector and investigation prompt. Runtime scans do NOT execute this.
